_comment: Committed connection config — never real secrets; the token is the ${CLOUDWAYS_ACCESS_TOKEN:-} env placeholder (tracked file). The connection authenticates when the variable is set: shell env on devices, the environment's env vars in claude.ai cloud sessions. The :- default keeps the config parseable when unset — the connection then just shows as unavailable in /mcp until the token is provided. Named cloudways-env so it never shadows a user-scope connection added via `claude mcp add -s user cloudways ...` (project scope wins over user scope on name collisions). Multi-account setups keep using `claude mcp add -s user cloudways-<client> ...` per references/installation.md.
